articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: Iran and Iranian-backed militias fired missiles at Israel and Arab states and Israel and the United States pounded targets in Iran as the war expanded Monday with statements of defiance and warnings of more U.S. casualties.
articles.title: Live updates: Iran will not negotiate with US, top Iranian security official says

articles.title: Live updates: War widens to include Iranian-backed militias as US and Israel pound targets in Iran

articles.author: Cara Anna
articles.description: The United States and Israel targeted Iran in coordinated attacks over the weekend that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and set off a furious Iranian response. Here's where things stand. There are concerns about a wider regional war after Iranian strikes hit Israel and U.S. military bases in the region as well as Gulf nations. Iranian officials are hurrying to select a new supreme leader for the first time since 1989. U.S. President Donald Trump has urged Iranians to seize the moment and overthrow the theocracy that cracked down on nationwide protests early this year.
articles.title: Here's where things stand after the US and Israel attacked Iran

articles.author: Jon Gambrell, David Rising, Samy Magdy
articles.description: Iran and Iranian-backed militias have fired missiles at Israel and Arab states, apparently hitting the U.S. Embassy compound in Kuwait, while Israel and the United States pounded targets in Iran. The war expanded on Monday with statements of defiance and increasing casualties. In Iran, the Red Crescent Society said the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes killed at least 555 people so far in the Islamic Republic. Kuwait’s defense ministry says several American warplanes have crashed in the country. The U.S. Embassy compound in Kuwait City was also hit. A drone attack targeted a Saudi oil refinery but officials say the drones were intercepted. The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah claimed strikes on Israel, which fired back, killing at least 31 people.
articles.title: Iran-backed militias join war, US Embassy in Kuwait is hit

articles.author: Michelle L. Price, Mary Clare Jalonick, Ben Finley, Josef Federman, David Klepper
articles.description: Trump administration officials told congressional staff in private briefings that U.S. intelligence did not suggest Iran was preparing to launch a preemptive strike against the U.S. That's according to three people familiar with Sunday's briefings who insisted on anonymity to discuss details that have not been made public. That information contrasts with the message from President Donald Trump, who said there were “imminent threats” from Iran. Two of the people said the administration officials also did not provide any clarity about what would happen next in Iran. The White House and Pentagon did not immediately reply to requests for comment on Sunday night.
articles.title: US intel did not suggest a preemptive strike from Iran, AP sources say

articles.author: Elaine Kurtenbach
articles.description: The U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran have sent a chill through world markets, with U.S. futures falling more than 1%. European markets opened lower early Monday and oil prices were up about 9%. The U.S. dollar rose against the Japanese yen and the euro. In Asian trading, shares slipped in Tokyo and most other markets but they rose in Shanghai, where higher oil prices lifted stocks in oil companies. War in the Middle East is expected to disrupt oil supplies. On Friday, the S&P 500 fell 0.4% to finish just its second losing month in the last 10. The Dow industrials dropped 1.1%, and the Nasdaq composite fell 0.9%.
articles.title: Oil prices soar and world shares drop as US-Israel war with Iran rattles markets

articles.author: Brian Melley
articles.description: Iranian missiles and drones have hit the United Arab Emirates, shaking Dubai’s image as a safe, tax-free haven for foreigners. Officials say debris from intercepted weapons sparked fires at major landmarks. A person was killed at Abu Dhabi’s airport, and seven others were injured. The Defense Ministry says air defenses had dealt with hundreds of missiles and drones over two days. Videos show fires near luxury hotels. Officials have praised their air defenses and say they will work hard to keep people safe. Visitors describe hearing constant blasts, and some say they will reconsider returning.
articles.title: Dubai's image as a haven is rocked by Iranian airstrikes

articles.author: Adam Geller, Abby Sewell
articles.description: As Israel unleashed a sweeping military response to the brutal assault by Hamas on October 7, 2023, it aimed punch after punch at Iran, the militant group’s longtime sponsor, and its proxies. The result has been a systematic and rapid degradation of Iran’s clout across the Middle East over the past 2½ years, a seismic change that lead directly to this weekend’s devastating attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel. Hezbollah has waded in on a small level, but how other proxy groups could react to Khamenei’s death remains to be seen.
articles.title: Series of blows to Iran and its proxies set the stage for US-Israel attacks
